Potato Growth Gallery
Growth Stages of Potato Plants
Variety Growth Galleries
Farming Best Practices
Explore the growth journey of our premium potato varieties from planting to harvest.
Understanding the growth cycle of potato plants helps farmers plan their activities and maximize yields. Each of our potato varieties goes through these four main stages, though the timing may vary.
The initial stage where potato seeds are planted and begin to sprout. This typically occurs 2-3 weeks after planting, when small green shoots emerge from the soil.
During this stage, the potato plants develop their foliage and grow rapidly. The plants establish strong root systems and begin storing energy for tuber formation.
Plants begin to flower, indicating the start of tuber formation beneath the soil. The flowers vary in color depending on the variety, from white to purple.
The final stage where potato plants begin to yellow and die back, signaling that the tubers are mature and ready for harvest.
Explore the growth journey of each of our potato varieties. See how they develop from planting to harvest, and learn about the unique characteristics of each variety.

About Waja Agrivantage Limited
Our Story
Our Locations
Ready to Partner with Us?
Your trusted partner in quality potato seed production, serving farmers across all 47 counties since 2022
Founded in 2022, Waja Agrivantage Limited has quickly established itself as a trusted provider of premium certified potato seeds in Kenya. Based in Nyahururu, Laikipia County, we are committed to supporting Kenyan farmers with high-quality seed potatoes that drive agricultural productivity and profitability.
What started as a vision to improve potato farming in Kenya has grown into a nationwide operation with 6 strategic collection points across Central Kenya, serving farmers in all 47 counties. Our network includes 3 own stores and partnerships with 3 trusted agrovets, ensuring convenient access to quality seeds wherever you are.
We specialize in Kenya's most popular and productive potato varieties including Shangi, Wanjiku, and Stephen, offering multiple seed classes from mini tubers to certified generations. Our commitment to quality, innovation, and farmer success has made us a preferred partner for both small-scale and commercial potato farmers across the country.
All our seeds are certified and rigorously tested for disease resistance, purity, and vigor, ensuring the best start for your crop.
We provide expert advice, flexible prebooking options, and comprehensive support to ensure your farming success from planting to harvest.
With 6 collection points in Central Kenya and delivery to all counties, we make quality seeds accessible to every Kenyan farmer.
